rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Not Of Mot And Mot For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Written Settlement Agreement; Mp&As; Decl. Of Kevin Whiteford
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN PART. DENY AS TO DISCRIMINATION. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER TERMINATION PRETEXTUAL. GEIST'S STRAY COMMENT, SHAW'S ADMISSION THAT GEIST HAD A "FEMALE ISSUE," AND GEIST'S CONDUCT TOWARD PLAINTIFF CREATES TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE DISCRIMINATION ANIMUS. GRANT AS TO HARASSMENT. DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT NOT SUFFICIENTLY PERVASIVE OR SEVERE TO CONSTITUTE SEXUAL HARASSMENT. DENY AS TO RETALIATION. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER TERMINATION PRETEXTUAL. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT PLAINTIFF COMPLAINED ABOUT GEIST'S CONDUCT, GEIST WAS UPSET ABOUT THE COMPLAINT, AND SHE WAS TERMINATED SHORTLY THEREAFTER. DENY AS TO FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT TOOK REASONABLE MEASURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION. (PB)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOTTA VELIZ VS. HOLLYWOOD ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: SUSTAIN DEMURRER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. (1)TO IDENTIFY THE TYPE OF CONTRACT, SEE CCP 430.10(G); (2)TO SEGREGATE DISTINCT CAUSES OF ACTION SEE CRC 312(G) AND (3)TO PLEAD VIOLATION OF CC 1671(D) AND 10504 OF THE COMMERCIAL CODE AS AN UNLAWFUL BUSINESS PRACTICE UNDER B&P 17200 OR IN A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF UNDER CCP 1060. VIOLATION OF B&P 17200 (3RD CAUSE OF ACTION) NEEDS TO PLEAD WITH MORE PARTICULARITY BY IDENTIFYING TYPE OF CONTRACT SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CA (1993) 14 CAL.APP.4TH 612, 614. (KS(EX))
